howIThink.title: How I engineer
howIThink.subtitle: From system design to production — with the rigor that financial systems demand.

steps.title: Design for failure
steps.body: Idempotency, circuit breakers, retry policies — financial systems assume the network will lie. I design for that from day one.

steps.title: Define the data contracts
steps.body: API schemas, event schemas, and database boundaries established before writing a line of business logic.

steps.title: Build observable systems
steps.body: Structured logging, distributed tracing, and SLOs tied to user-visible outcomes — not vanity metrics.

steps.title: Harden for compliance
steps.body: Security, audit trails, and regulatory constraints as first-class design inputs — not post-launch patches.

howIThink.operatingBody: Financial infrastructure is not a CRUD app. It is a system of guarantees — about consistency, about recoverability, about what the user's money does when the network fails.
bullets: Idempotency and exactly-once semantics as default design constraints
Audit logs and transaction traces as core features, not afterthoughts
Security and compliance designed in, not bolted on

featuredProjects.title: Featured projects
featuredProjects.heading: Case studies built for enterprise credibility.
featuredProjects.subtitle: Investor-ready narratives — problem, architecture, security, scale, and measurable outcomes.
featuredProjects.bridgeSubtitle: Share the goal and hard constraints — I will outline options, risks, and a realistic first step.

items.title: Pan-African Payment SDK
items.problem: Products needed separate integrations for Orange Money, Wave, and Free Money — slow launches and inconsistent error handling across providers.
items.solution: Unified SDK exposing one API for initiate, confirm, and reconcile flows — provider-specific adapters behind a stable contract.
items.architecture: Adapter pattern with Spring Boot services, Redis idempotency keys, and Kafka events for async settlement notifications.
items.security: Signed requests, webhook HMAC validation, and per-tenant API keys with scoped permissions.
items.scale: Horizontally scaled stateless API; provider latency isolated via async queues without blocking client threads.
items.impact: ~60% faster integration time for new wallets; single observability surface across 3+ live providers.

items.title: Double-Entry Ledger System
items.problem: Financial products required auditable balances with strict consistency — spreadsheet-style tracking was not acceptable for compliance.
items.solution: High-consistency ledger with double-entry postings, immutable audit log, and transactional guarantees on every money movement.
items.architecture: PostgreSQL with serializable transactions for postings; append-only audit store; reconciliation jobs for provider drift.
items.security: Role-based access, immutable audit trail, and no destructive updates on posted entries.
items.scale: Partition-friendly schema and batch reconciliation for high-volume mobile-money corridors.
items.impact: Full auditability for regulators and finance teams; zero balance discrepancies in production reconciliation.

items.title: Unified API Gateway
items.problem: A bank and its partners needed one entry point to African payment providers without exposing fragile core systems.
items.solution: Enterprise gateway centralizing auth, routing, rate limits, and provider orchestration behind versioned REST APIs.
items.architecture: Spring Cloud Gateway + domain microservices; Kafka for domain events; Jasper/reporting decoupled from transactional path.
items.security: JWT at edge, RBAC, input validation before core contact, full request audit logging.
items.scale: Stateless gateway tier; Kafka absorbs analytics peaks without blocking payments.
items.impact: 3 partner integrations live; ~40% faster partner onboarding; 12+ months zero-downtime deploys.

items.title: School Management SaaS
items.problem: Private schools ran disconnected admin, billing, and academic tools — no single operational source of truth.
items.solution: Modern multi-tenant SaaS for admissions, billing, staff, and reporting — built for African school operations.
items.architecture: Angular + Spring Boot REST; async jobs for bulk operations; modular domains per school tenant.
items.security: Tenant isolation, encrypted sensitive records, role-based staff access.
items.scale: Async processing for reports and notifications; shared platform serving growing school networks.
items.impact: ~35% less admin handoff time; unified records across departments.

architectureSection.title: System architecture
architectureSection.subtitle: How I think about and build distributed financial backend systems — from API gateway to data layer.
principles: Idempotency by default — every write operation is safe to retry
Event sourcing for financial state — full audit trail, always
Eventual consistency where safe, strong consistency where money moves
Security at every layer — not a single perimeter

about.title: About
about.p1: I am an independent backend and fintech engineer focused on payment systems, distributed backends, and enterprise APIs — built for Africa's realities and trusted by international clients who need production-grade delivery.
about.p2a: Beyond writing code, I contribute to
about.p2b: system architecture, API design, engineering standards, and junior engineer mentorship
about.p2c: — the work that keeps teams shipping reliably when complexity and traffic grow.
about.p3: Strong Java & Spring Boot background, event-driven architectures with Kafka, and Angular/TypeScript for full-stack delivery when needed.
about.p4: Based in Dakar, Senegal (GMT). Available for international freelance and contract engagements. Long-term interest: building financial infrastructure products from the ground up.
